channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Massachusetts Democrat says she was targeted by bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Lori Tahan, D-Mass., shared that she and her family were the target of a bomb threat on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: National nonprofit asks Gov. McMaster to 'intervene' in South Carolina
item.description: A controversial Texas-based nonprofit promising to set up citizen patrols in McColl is now asking for state help.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with Macron, Zelenskyy ahead of Notre Dame reopening ceremony in Paris
item.description: President-elect Trump visited with French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy ahead of the Notre Dame reopening ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump nominee battles brew, NC Senate cleared of raucous onlookers
item.description: Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson cleared the North Carolina Senate chamber this week for the second time in recent days amid protestations against the override of Gov. Cooper's veto on an election board appointments law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: Four Democrats are running for Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bureau of Prisons agrees to court monitor, public acknowledgement of staff-on-inmate sexual abuse
item.description: A legal settlement in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse will force the federal Bureau of Prisons to open its doors to a court-appointed monitor and publicly acknowledge pervasive misconduct at its now-shuttered women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pete Hegseth says he had ‘substantive conversation’ with Joni Ernst as Trump signals support
item.description: President-elect Trump threw his support behind embattled defense secretary pick Pete Hegseth Friday as the nominee continues to meet with Republican senators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Military leaders are rattled by a list of 'woke' officers that a group urges Hegseth to fire
item.description: The list compiled by the American Accountability Foundation includes 20 general officers or senior admirals and a disproportionate number of female officers.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump: Does he hew to the institutional norms he’s spent decades defending or flex the powers of the presidency in untested ways? Th
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son sparks calls for granting more clemencies
item.description: President Biden’s surprise pardon of his son Hunter has prompted calls for the outgoing commander in chief to grant clemency to other Americans before he hands over the White House to President-elect Trump next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin McCarthy: Gaetz lied to Trump before AG nomination
item.description: Should the House Ethics Committee still release its report on Gaetz, even if he’s not going to be part of Trump’s Cabinet?
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nadler bows out of Judiciary race, will endorse Raskin
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) is ceding his position as top Democrat on the powerful Judiciary Committee amid a tough challenge from Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), — a stunning shift on a powerful committee that’s sure to play a considerable role in the oversight of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves to toss Georgia criminal case as president-elect
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday asked to throw out his criminal election interference charges in Georgia following his election victory. Trump’s two federal indictments have already been tossed, and the former president is attempting to do the same in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: Gail Slater will take over the DOJ's high-profile antitrust cases against tech giants like Google and Apple.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Montana lawmakers cross the aisle to block a trans bathroom ban in the state capitol
item.description: Some Republicans joined Democrats in rejecting the measure, which would have restricted bathroom access. Rep. Zooey Zephyr, Montana's first openly transgender legislator, won a second term last month.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrats stick with Schumer as leader
item.description: Chuck Schumer faced no opposition in the party leadership elections, in which Illinois Sen. Dick Durbin was also re-elected to the No. 2 spot and Minnesota Sen. Amy Klobuchar became the new No. 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The fight against medical debt is pivoting to the states after Trump's election
item.description: Patient and consumer advocates fear a new Trump administration will scale back federal efforts to expand financial protections for patients and shield them from debt.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Omar Fateh announces candidacy for Minneapolis mayor, says Frey is ‘failing’ residents
item.description: The first Somali elected to the Minnesota Senate is now seeking to lead the states largest city. Sen. Omar Fateh announced Monday he will challenge incumbent, and fellow DFLer Jacob Frey for the mayor’s office.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
